golden_rule.title: The Golden Rule: Serverless for Iteration, Pods Only for Final Submission
golden_rule.description: Use serverless for development, testing, and architecture validation. It's cheaper, faster, and auto-stops. But
golden_rule.highlight: official PR submissions must run on 8×H100 SXM with verifiable timing
golden_rule.description_suffix: — serverless providers may use different hardware or add overhead that affects your reported step times. Use serverless to iterate, then do your final 3-seed submission run on a known 8×H100 SXM pod.

providers.name: RunPod Serverless
providers.description: Submit job → it runs → results come back → billing stops. No SSH, no pod management.
providers.note: H100 SXM. ~$0.45 per 10-min training run (calculated, not yet verified via receipt).

providers.name: Modal
providers.description: Serverless by default. modal run train.py — that's it. $30 free every month = multiple runs.

providers.name: Google Colab
providers.description: GPUs available: T4 (free), A100 40/80GB, H100, RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell ~96GB (varies by tier and availability). Student Pro access available through educational institutions.
providers.note: Whether Colab's H100 is SXM or PCIe variant is unverified. Validate architecture on Colab, verify final submission timing on known SXM hardware.
providers.tips: Batch size fix for single GPU: TRAIN_BATCH_TOKENS=65536. Save checkpoints to Drive. Resume on disconnect. ~6-7 hours for 20K steps on single H100.

smoke_tests.name: Quick forward pass
smoke_tests.catches: Import errors, shape mismatches, config bugs

smoke_tests.name: Full mini-run
smoke_tests.command: ITERATIONS=50 TRAIN_BATCH_TOKENS=4096 TRAIN_SEQ_LEN=256 VAL_LOSS_EVERY=25 python train_gpt.py

hardware_comparison.title: H100 SXM vs PCIe
hardware_comparison.note: Competition requires 8×H100 SXM for official timing. Colab H100 variant unverified — always confirm step timing matches expected SXM performance before submitting.
